fleet.description: Fleet related configuration. Fleets are a Google Cloud concept for logically organizing clusters, letting you use and manage multi-cluster capabilities and apply consistent policies across your systems.
fleet.rationale: Proper fleet configuration ensures clusters are organized in a secure and manageable way with consistent policy enforcement.
fleet.compliant: Properly formatted project reference using project number
fleet.non-compliant: Hardcoded project numbers or incorrect formatting

project.description: The name of the Fleet host project where this cluster will be registered. Project names are formatted as `projects/<project-number>`.
project.rationale: Correct project reference format ensures proper cluster registration and management within the intended fleet.
project.non-compliant: projects/gdce-dev (using project ID instead of number)

membership.description: (Output) The name of the managed Hub Membership resource associated to this cluster. Membership names are formatted as `projects/<project-number>/locations/global/membership/<cluster-id>`.
membership.rationale: Output-only field used for tracking cluster membership, no security impact.

networking.description: Network configuration for the cluster including CIDR blocks for pods and services.
networking.rationale: Proper network segmentation prevents unauthorized access and contains potential security breaches.

cluster_ipv4_cidr_blocks.description: All pods in the cluster are assigned an RFC1918 IPv4 address from these blocks. Only a single block is supported. This field cannot be changed after creation.
cluster_ipv4_cidr_blocks.rationale: Properly scoped CIDR blocks prevent overly permissive network access between pods.

services_ipv4_cidr_blocks.description: All services in the cluster are assigned an RFC1918 IPv4 address from these blocks. Only a single block is supported. This field cannot be changed after creation.
services_ipv4_cidr_blocks.rationale: Properly scoped CIDR blocks prevent overly permissive network access to services.

cluster_ipv6_cidr_blocks.description: If specified, dual stack mode is enabled and all pods in the cluster are assigned an IPv6 address from these blocks alongside from an IPv4 address. Only a single block is supported. This field cannot be changed after creation.
cluster_ipv6_cidr_blocks.rationale: IPv6 CIDR blocks should follow proper network segmentation principles similar to IPv4.

services_ipv6_cidr_blocks.description: If specified, dual stack mode is enabled and all services in the cluster are assigned an IPv6 address from these blocks alongside from an IPv4 address. Only a single block is supported. This field cannot be changed after creation.
services_ipv6_cidr_blocks.rationale: IPv6 service CIDR blocks should follow proper network segmentation principles.

network_type.description: (Output) IP addressing type of this cluster i.e. SINGLESTACK_V4 vs DUALSTACK_V4_V6.
network_type.rationale: Output-only field indicating network configuration type, no security impact.

authorization.description: RBAC policy that will be applied and managed by GEC.
authorization.rationale: Proper RBAC configuration ensures only authorized users have administrative access to the cluster.
authorization.compliant: Authorized admin users with proper email addresses
authorization.non-compliant: Unauthorized or invalid admin email addresses

admin_users.description: User that will be granted the cluster-admin role on the cluster, providing full access to the cluster. Currently, this is a singular field, but will be expanded to allow multiple admins in the future.
admin_users.rationale: Admin users should be properly vetted and authorized individuals with valid organizational email addresses.

location.rationale: Location selection impacts data residency, latency, and compliance with regional regulations.

name.rationale: Cluster name is an identifier and doesn't directly impact security.

labels.description: User-defined labels for the edgecloud cluster. **Note**: This field is non-authoritative, and will only manage the labels present in your configuration. Please refer to the field `effective_labels` for all of the labels present on the resource.
labels.rationale: Proper labeling ensures resources can be properly identified, tracked, and managed according to organizational policies.
labels.compliant: Contains required labels: owner, environment, cost-center
labels.non-compliant: Missing required labels or using non-standard label names

default_max_pods_per_node.description: The default maximum number of pods per node used if a maximum value is not specified explicitly for a node pool in this cluster. If unspecified, the Kubernetes default value will be used.
default_max_pods_per_node.rationale: Excessive pods per node can lead to resource contention and reduced security isolation between workloads.

maintenance_policy.rationale: Proper maintenance windows ensure cluster updates occur during approved times with minimal business impact.
maintenance_policy.compliant: Defined maintenance window with proper recurrence pattern

window.description: Specifies the maintenance window in which maintenance may be performed.
window.rationale: Maintenance windows should be scheduled during low-usage periods with proper recurrence patterns.

recurring_window.description: Represents an arbitrary window of time that recurs.
recurring_window.rationale: Recurring windows provide predictable maintenance schedules that can be planned around.

maintenance_exclusions.description: Exclusions to automatic maintenance. Non-emergency maintenance should not occur in these windows. Each exclusion has a unique name and may be active or expired. The max number of maintenance exclusions allowed at a given time is 3.
maintenance_exclusions.rationale: Maintenance exclusions prevent updates during critical business periods but should be used judiciously.
maintenance_exclusions.compliant: Properly defined exclusions with unique IDs

id.description: A unique (per cluster) id for the window.
id.rationale: Unique IDs ensure proper tracking and management of maintenance exclusions.

control_plane.description: The configuration of the cluster control plane.
control_plane.rationale: Control plane configuration impacts cluster stability, performance, and security isolation.
control_plane.compliant: Properly configured control plane with appropriate node count and location
control_plane.non-compliant: Misconfigured or insecure control plane settings

remote.rationale: Remote control plane location impacts latency, availability, and data residency.

node_location.description: Name of the Google Distributed Cloud Edge zones where this node pool will be created. For example: `us-central1-edge-customer-a`.
node_location.rationale: Node location should be in approved zones that meet data residency and performance requirements.

local.rationale: Local control plane configuration impacts high availability, resource isolation, and deployment policies.
local.compliant: Proper node count (1 or 3) with appropriate machine filtering
local.non-compliant: Invalid node count or overly permissive machine filters

node_location.description: Name of the Google Distributed Cloud Edge zones where this node pool will be created. For example: `us-central1-edge-customer-a`.
node_location.rationale: Node location should be in approved zones that meet data residency and performance requirements.

node_count.description: The number of nodes to serve as replicas of the Control Plane. Only 1 and 3 are supported.
node_count.rationale: Proper node count ensures high availability and fault tolerance for the control plane.

machine_filter.description: Only machines matching this filter will be allowed to host control plane nodes. The filtering language accepts strings like "name=<name>", and is documented here: [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160).
machine_filter.rationale: Machine filters ensure control plane nodes run only on approved, properly configured hardware.

shared_deployment_policy.description: Policy configuration about how user applications are deployed. Possible values are: `SHARED_DEPLOYMENT_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED`, `ALLOWED`, `DISALLOWED`.
shared_deployment_policy.rationale: Shared deployment policy controls whether user applications can run on control plane nodes, impacting security isolation.

system_addons_config.description: Config that customers are allowed to define for GDCE system add-ons.
system_addons_config.rationale: System addons configuration impacts cluster functionality and security posture.
system_addons_config.compliant: Properly configured addons with secure settings

ingress.description: Config for the Ingress add-on which allows customers to create an Ingress object to manage external access to the servers in a cluster. The add-on consists of istiod and istio-ingress.
ingress.rationale: Ingress configuration controls external access to cluster services and should be properly secured.
ingress.compliant: Properly configured ingress with secure VIP settings
ingress.non-compliant: Misconfigured or overly permissive ingress settings

disabled.rationale: Disabling ingress when not needed reduces attack surface.
disabled.non-compliant: false (when external access not properly secured)

ipv4_vip.rationale: Ingress VIP should use properly scoped IP addresses to prevent unauthorized access.

external_load_balancer_ipv4_address_pool.description: Address pools for cluster data plane external load balancing.
external_load_balancer_ipv4_address_pool.rationale: Load balancer address pools should use properly scoped IP ranges to prevent unauthorized access.
external_load_balancer_ipv4_address_pool.non-compliant: Overly permissive or public IP ranges

control_plane_encryption.description: Remote control plane disk encryption options. This field is only used when enabling CMEK support.
control_plane_encryption.rationale: Control plane encryption protects sensitive cluster data at rest and should use customer-managed keys when required.
control_plane_encryption.non-compliant: Missing encryption or misconfigured KMS keys

kms_key.description: The Cloud KMS CryptoKey e.g. projects/{project}/locations/{location}/keyRings/{keyRing}/cryptoKeys/{cryptoKey} to use for protecting control plane disks. If not specified, a Google-managed key will be used instead.
kms_key.rationale: Customer-managed keys provide better control over encryption and access policies.
kms_key.non-compliant: Missing or improperly formatted KMS key reference

kms_key_active_version.description: (Output) The Cloud KMS CryptoKeyVersion currently in use for protecting control plane disks. Only applicable if kms_key is set.
kms_key_active_version.rationale: Output-only field indicating current key version, no security impact.

kms_key_state.description: (Output) Availability of the Cloud KMS CryptoKey. If not `KEY_AVAILABLE`, then nodes may go offline as they cannot access their local data. This can be caused by a lack of permissions to use the key, or if the key is disabled or deleted.
kms_key_state.rationale: Output-only field indicating key status, no security impact.

kms_status.description: (Output) Error status returned by Cloud KMS when using this key. This field may be populated only if `kms_key_state` is not `KMS_KEY_STATE_KEY_AVAILABLE`. If populated, this field contains the error status reported by Cloud KMS.
kms_status.rationale: Output-only field for KMS error status, no security impact.

target_version.description: The target cluster version. For example: "1.5.0".
target_version.rationale: Cluster version should be kept current to ensure security patches and bug fixes are applied.

release_channel.description: The release channel a cluster is subscribed to. Possible values are: `RELEASE_CHANNEL_UNSPECIFIED`, `NONE`, `REGULAR`.
release_channel.rationale: Release channel selection impacts stability and security update frequency.

project.description: If it is not provided, the provider project is used.
project.rationale: Project selection impacts resource isolation, billing, and access controls.

admin_users.description: Administrative users configuration for the cluster.
admin_users.rationale: Admin users should be properly authorized individuals with valid organizational credentials.

username.rationale: Admin usernames should be valid organizational accounts with proper authorization.

window.rationale: Proper maintenance windows ensure updates occur during approved times with minimal business impact.

recurring_window.description: Represents an arbitrary window of time that recurs.
recurring_window.rationale: Recurring windows provide predictable maintenance schedules that can be planned around.
Proper recurring window configuration ensures maintenance occurs predictably and during approved times.

window.description: Represents an arbitrary window of time.
window.rationale: Window timing should align with business low-usage periods to minimize impact.
window.compliant: Proper start and end times during low-usage periods

recurrence.description: An RRULE (https://tools.ietf.org/html/rfc5545#section-3.8.5.3) for how this window recurs. They go on for the span of time between the start and end time.
recurrence.rationale: Proper recurrence patterns ensure maintenance occurs at predictable intervals.

maintenance_exclusions.rationale: Maintenance exclusions prevent updates during critical business periods but should be used judiciously.
maintenance_exclusions.compliant: Properly defined exclusions with unique IDs

window.description: Represents an arbitrary window of time.
window.rationale: Exclusion windows should be properly defined to prevent maintenance during critical periods.

id.description: A unique (per cluster) id for the window.
id.rationale: Unique IDs ensure proper tracking and management of maintenance exclusions.

remote.rationale: Remote control plane configuration impacts where control plane components run and their network accessibility.
remote.compliant: Properly configured remote control plane in approved locations
remote.non-compliant: Misconfigured or unapproved remote control plane settings

node_location.description: Name of the Google Distributed Cloud Edge zones where this node pool will be created. For example: `us-central1-edge-customer-a`.
node_location.rationale: Remote node location should be in approved zones that meet security and compliance requirements.

local.rationale: Local control plane configuration impacts high availability, resource isolation, and security boundaries.
local.compliant: Properly configured local control plane with appropriate security settings
local.non-compliant: Misconfigured or insecure local control plane settings

node_location.description: Name of the Google Distributed Cloud Edge zones where this node pool will be created. For example: `us-central1-edge-customer-a`.
node_location.rationale: Local node location should be in approved zones that meet security and compliance requirements.

node_count.description: The number of nodes to serve as replicas of the Control Plane. Only 1 and 3 are supported.
node_count.rationale: Proper node count ensures high availability and fault tolerance for the control plane.

machine_filter.description: Only machines matching this filter will be allowed to host control plane nodes. The filtering language accepts strings like "name=<name>", and is documented here: [AIP-160](https://google.aip.dev/160).
machine_filter.rationale: Machine filters ensure control plane nodes run only on approved, properly configured hardware with adequate security controls.
machine_filter.non-compliant: Overly permissive filters or missing filters

shared_deployment_policy.description: Policy configuration about how user applications are deployed. Possible values are: `SHARED_DEPLOYMENT_POLICY_UNSPECIFIED`, `ALLOWED`, `DISALLOWED`.
shared_deployment_policy.rationale: Shared deployment policy controls whether user applications can run on control plane nodes, impacting security isolation and attack surface.

ingress.description: Ingress add-on configuration for external access to cluster services.
ingress.rationale: Ingress configuration controls external access to cluster services and significantly impacts the cluster's security posture.
ingress.non-compliant: Misconfigured or overly permissive ingress settings

disabled.rationale: Disabling ingress when not needed reduces the attack surface and prevents unauthorized external access.
disabled.non-compliant: false (when external access is not properly secured)

ipv4_vip.rationale: Ingress VIP should use properly scoped IP addresses within approved ranges to prevent unauthorized access.
